questions.text: Which of the following Harappan archaeological sites is famous for the discovery of a 'ploughed field'?
Who among the following Delhi Sultans is known for the controversial experiment of introducing 'Token Currency' (copper/brass coins) in India?
The 'Servants of India Society' was founded in 1905 with the objective of training national missionaries for the service of India. Who was its founder?
Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to take steps to organise village panchayats and endow them with necessary powers?
The feature of 'Suspension of Fundamental Rights during Emergency' in the Indian Constitution was borrowed from the constitution of which country?
Which of the following Indian states has the longest mainland coastline?
The 'Great Victoria Desert', known for its numerous salt lakes and grassland plains, is located in which country?
The river known as 'Yarlung Tsangpo' in Tibet enters India through Arunachal Pradesh. By what name is it primarily known in India?
In economics, which curve illustrates the relationship between tax rates and the amount of tax revenue collected by governments?
Who among the following serves as the ex-officio Chairman of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council in India?
options: C. Governor of the Reserve Bank of India

questions.text: Which part of the human eye is responsible for controlling the size of the pupil and thus regulating the amount of light entering the eye?
What is the chemical name of the compound commonly known as 'Laughing Gas'?
In the International System of Units (SI), what is the unit of measurement for Electrical Capacitance?
The highly rhythmic 'Cheraw' dance, often characterized by the use of bamboo staves, is a traditional cultural dance of which state?
The traditional buffalo race known as 'Kambala' is an annual sporting event held primarily in the coastal districts of which Indian state?
Who among the following is the author of the critically acclaimed and voluminous English novel 'A Suitable Boy'?
Where is the headquarters of the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), a specialized agency of the United Nations, located?
The severe nutritional disorder known as 'Kwashiorkor', which primarily affects young children, is caused by a severe deficiency of:
By which Constitutional Amendment Act was the 'Tenth Schedule' added to the Indian Constitution, thereby introducing the Anti-Defection Law?
The historic 'Gandhi-Irwin Pact', which led to the suspension of the Civil Disobedience Movement, was signed in which year?
Which of the following mountain passes forms a vital strategic link connecting the Indian state of Sikkim with the Tibet Autonomous Region of China?
Which of the following highly reactive metals is kept safely immersed in kerosene oil to prevent it from reacting with oxygen and catching fire?
In the context of the banking and financial sector in India, what is the full form of the widely used acronym 'NPA'?
The sports terminology 'Tee', which refers to a small peg used to elevate the ball before taking the first stroke, is associated with which sport?
Which of the following scientific instruments is used specifically to measure the humidity (amount of water vapor) present in the atmosphere?
